why: Closed contract (additionalProperties:false) is PERMANENT (D2): an unknown top-level key ALWAYS rejects. New metadata goes in `extensions` or a new schema-versioned field, never an ad-hoc key.
The sovereignty object is CLOSED (D2). An unknown sub-field (`region`) rejects — the envelope's passport block admits no undeclared keys.
source is one of the six REQUIRED fields (§6). Absence rejects; there is no default source (the fabricated-default class, cortex#1812).
payload MUST be a JSON object (§3.6); the reference validator rejects arrays and null. An array payload rejects.
id MUST match the 8-4-4-4-12 hex `uuid` grammar (§3.1). A non-hex, wrong-shape string rejects. D7 keeps the grammar version-AGNOSTIC but still requires the canonical shape.

D7: a `urn:uuid:` PREFIX is REJECTED — the `uuid` rule has no prefix production. cortex's ajv-formats currently ACCEPTS this prefix (a value valid at cortex, rejected here); the divergence is pinned and tightens onto this rule at flag-day R.

D8 STRICT RFC 3339: the `T` separator and `Z` zulu designator are UPPERCASE-ONLY (ABNF %s"T"/%s"Z"; the source regex has no /i). Lowercase `t`/`z` reject. cortex's ajv-formats is case-insensitive here — the pinned divergence.

MOVED from the accept oracle by D8 (RFC §12: a resolved decision moves the vector, keeping its id). Strict RFC 3339 now REQUIRES a calendar-valid finite instant: month 02 has no day 30, and hour 25 / minute-second 99 are out of range. The myelin reference ISO8601_RE did no calendar check and once ACCEPTED this — that acceptance is retired.

D16: source is a FULL agent DID (did:mf:agent.{principal}.{stack}.{assistant}) — agent-msi has EXACTLY three segments after the tag. A fourth segment rejects (arity mismatch). The pre-cut loose 4-5 segment `org.agent.instance` source is gone.
D16: source is pinned to the AGENT class. A well-formed principal-class DID is still not a valid source — the origin address names the emitting assistant, not a bare principal.
D10: `type` is 2-5 kebab-strict segments (domain.entity.action). A single segment rejects — the 2-5 count is envelope-law even though it imports RFC-0001's segment alphabet.

MOVED from the accept oracle by D6 (RFC §12). At flag-day R signed_by is ARRAY-ONLY; the pre-#31 single-object shim is retired and now rejects. This also removes the getActorIdentity shim-form defect at its root.

TWO-PLANE REJECT (D15/D24): a SELF-ASSERTED-class DID (surface) holds no key and MUST NOT appear in signed_by[]. Enforced at both schema-pattern and verify time (RFC-0001 §2.1, cited by RFC-0004 §5). Reject half of the pair whose accepts are envelope/originator-adapter-resolved and envelope/originator-system-class.
A stamp MUST NOT carry the legacy `principal` key (dropped by the myelin#182 R2 breaking cut); the canonical DID key is `identity`. Each stamp object is closed — the legacy key rejects as unknown.

The stamp signature has schema minLength 88 (base64-signature). A 40-char signature rejects at THIS layer. Canonical exactly-88 / non-malleability is RFC-0004's `signature` (§4), onto which this tightens at R.

D20: target_assistant is an AGENT-class DID only — it names the receiving assistant, never a principal. A principal-class DID rejects even though it is a well-formed DID and the cross-field rule is otherwise satisfied.
The legacy key `target_principal` was removed by the R13 breaking cut and is reserved-as-removed: an envelope carrying it rejects as an unknown field (closed contract, D2).
Cross-field rule (§6): distribution_mode ∈ {direct,delegate} ⇒ target_assistant REQUIRED. `direct` with no target rejects. Accept half is envelope/direct-with-target.

distribution_mode is one of {offer,direct,delegate}. `broadcast` was removed from the wire by the R11 (#180) breaking cut and MUST be rejected — the stale schema `description` and docs prose that still bless it are defects (§8).
originator is exactly {identity, attribution}, closed. The legacy `principal` key (removed by the R2 breaking cut) rejects as an unknown field.

requirements items MUST match `capability-tag`: runs of alnum joined by a SINGLE '-', no consecutive '--'. `a--b` rejects. (RFC-0002's namespace.md states a looser grammar — a cross-doc divergence RFC-0002 must reconcile.)

D11: the whole-envelope receive bound is 1,048,576 octets (1 MiB). This envelope serializes to 1048640 octets (> the bound) and MUST be rejected receive-side. The transport-alignment sentence lives in RFC-0007; the numeric bound is envelope-law here. Pad is machine-generated 'a' repetition (confidentiality-gate safe).
